title: Washing machine descaler tablets
subtitle: Citric acid and bicarbonate-of-soda tablets for monthly drum and seal descaling
excerpt: Drop one tablet into the drum and run a hot empty cycle. Citric acid dissolves limescale from the drum, door seal, and heating element; bicarbonate of soda deodourises and neutralises any residual acid. A monthly tablet keeps hard-water scale from building up.
sourceNotes: Citric acid as a descaling agent: standard descaler chemistry. Safe use in domestic appliances: manufacturer-equivalent recommendations for citric acid descaling.

recipe.batchNotes: A batch of 10 to 12 tablets covers 2 to 3 months of monthly use. Scale up freely.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Press the tablets and leave to set for at least an hour before storing. They harden fully within 24 hours.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keep in a sealed container away from moisture. Use within one year. Discard if the tablets crumble to powder on handling or smell musty.

glossaryTerms.definition: Fine crystalline organic acid used as a descaler and in bath-bomb recipes. Dissolves calcium carbonate limescale on contact. Food-grade is safe for appliance cleaning.

content.text: Hard-water limescale coats the heating element, drum, and door seal of a washing machine over time, reducing efficiency and leaving a musty smell on clothes. Monthly descaling prevents build-up. These tablets use
to dissolve calcium carbonate deposits and bicarbonate of soda to deodourise the drum. One tablet per cycle is enough.

items.prepNote: about 10 ml; just enough to bind
optional, for scent during the cycle

content.text: Weigh out 150 g of citric acid and 50 g of bicarbonate of soda into a mixing bowl. Stir together with a dry spoon. Do not mix vigorously with water present, or the two will react and fizz. The citric acid and bicarb react slowly when dry -- this is expected and fine.

Add the essential oil if using. Then add 2 teaspoons of distilled water, a few drops at a time, stirring quickly between additions. The mixture should just clump together when pressed in your hand -- it will feel like damp sand. Add no more water than necessary; too much triggers the fizzing reaction before the tablets are formed.

Press the mixture firmly into the sections of a silicone mould, packing it down hard. Overfill each section slightly, then press a flat surface across the top to compact it. Leave in the mould for at least one hour, then unmould carefully. The tablets harden fully within 24 hours as they dry.

Drop one tablet into the empty washing machine drum. Run a hot cycle at 60°C or 90°C with no laundry and no detergent. Do not add to the detergent drawer -- the tablet works by dissolving in the drum water. Run a short rinse cycle afterwards to flush any residue. Use once a month in hard-water areas; every six weeks in soft-water areas.
Store in a sealed airtight tin or jar, away from moisture. Humidity causes the tablets to soften and lose their form. Use within one year. Discard any tablets that crumble when handled.
